bug_report.summary: View Source drag-selection does not extend across multiple lines (selection stops early, not reaching LINE5-DDD)
steps_to_reproduce: Open a local HTML file containing distinct markers on separate lines (e.g., LINE2-AAA … LINE6-EEE).
Click near the middle of the line containing `LINE2-AAA` and drag down to the middle of the line containing `LINE5-DDD`.
expected_behaviors: Selection highlight should visibly span from within `LINE2-AAA` through within `LINE5-DDD` (covering intermediate lines without gaps/jumps).
actual_behaviors: Selection highlight only covers `LINE2-AAA` and `LINE3-BBB` and does not extend to `LINE4-CCC`/`LINE5-DDD`, indicating the drag-selection is truncated at/near a line boundary.

false_positive_reasons: GUI element location or interaction error (non-deterministic behavior)

bug_report.summary: View Source: clicking does not clear/collapse an existing selection (selection highlight remains)
steps_to_reproduce: Open View Page Source (Ctrl+U) for any page.
Create a text selection in the view-source document (e.g., by dragging across lines).
Click once on empty whitespace in the page, or click inside a line of source text to place the caret.
expected_behaviors: The selection should be cleared/collapsed and the caret should move to the clicked location (no multi-line highlight remains).
actual_behaviors: The prior selection highlight remains visible after clicking (e.g., `LINE2-AAA`/`LINE3-BBB` stay highlighted), indicating the selection is not cleared/collapsed.

false_positive_reasons: LLM hallucination — misinterpreted or imagined GUI rendering

bug_report.summary: View Source: triple-click on a source line selects only a word/token instead of the whole line
steps_to_reproduce: Open an HTML file and open View Page Source (Ctrl+U).
In the view-source content, triple-click on a line with plain text (e.g., the line containing `LINE2-AAA`).
expected_behaviors: Triple-click should select the entire line (line selection behavior).
actual_behaviors: Only the `LINE2-AAA` token/word is highlighted/selected rather than the whole line.

false_positive_reasons: Unreasonable or unnecessary improvement suggestion, not a real issue

bug_report.summary: Find-in-Page bar shows stray extra field/text ('es') on View Source page after navigating matches
steps_to_reproduce: Open a local file in Firefox using a view-source:file:///... URL.
Press Ctrl+F to open Find in Page.
Press Enter to navigate to the next match.
expected_behaviors: Find bar should display the match counter text normally (e.g., "2 of 2 matches") with no extra/blank text fields or stray trailing characters.
actual_behaviors: An extra long, textbox-like blank area appears on the far right of the Find bar containing stray text ("es"), suggesting the match counter/status UI is mis-rendered or overlapped.

false_positive_reasons: LLM hallucination — misinterpreted or imagined GUI rendering
